filename: retrieving ROIs if deleted from Lola.docx
relative_path: tCycIF projects/Instructions/Antibodies and scanning/retrieving ROIs if deleted from Lola.docx
full_text:
Steps to retrieve ROIs and focus points based on previous .rcjob files

When scanning, the instrument should always generate an .rcjob file that summarizes the metadata regarding all of the settings and parameters used during the scan. This file is highly valuable and should always be saved and stored alongside the images produced, since it can be used to retrieve data removed from the scanner computer.

In the case of losing/deleting the scan area and/or focus points of a sample, go through the following steps:

In Cythub, create a new scan task for the slide ID you want to do another cycle on (as if you were doing this for the first time)

Initiate the first pass scan of the slide and make sure to specify in the settings a manual edit and “redetect” of the scanning area before going for the full scan.

Once the first pass has been done, go to the .roi file produced (located in rcpnl/rois folder).

Now, locate the last .rcjob file you have of any previous scan you performed on your slide.

Open the .rcjob file with Notepad and in the document look for the coordenates of the focus points, the X and Y start positions, and the scan width and height.

Go to the .roi file that was generated in step 2, whose location is sepcified in step 3

Open the .roi file and substitute the values for the focus points, startPosX, startPosY, scanHeightUm and scanWidthUm with the ones from the .rcjob file you had and save it. (Remove any extra focus points coordenates in the .roi file if the .rcjob file had less)

Go back to Cytehub and in the “SCAN AREA” tab look for the sample ID to locate the edited scanning area. It should now appear with the focus points and coordenates that you copied from the .rcjob file. (If not, make sure you saved it correctly, refresh Cytehub or click on the option to show all scans)

Once you made sure that the focus points and scanning area are the same as before, register the scan area. This should automatically initiate the scanning.

For more information contact pablo.siliceo@helsinki.fi
